# Verify that sesam supports age plugin identities and recipients end-to-end.
# Uses the mock age-plugin-sesamtest binary (registered in TestMain) so the
# scenario exercises the real plugin protocol without needing a YubiKey.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Tell: add 'yubi' as a dev with the mock plugin's recipient. ParseRecipient
# must dispatch the `age1sesamtest1…` HRP to the plugin client, and the
# reseal at the end of tell calls Wrap through the plugin.
exec sesam tell --user yubi --recipient $PLUGIN_PUBKEY --group dev

# Audit log now records the plugin recipient verbatim.
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"yubi"'
stdout 'age1sesamtest1'

# The plugin identity can decrypt the audit log: ParseIdentity dispatches
# AGE-PLUGIN-SESAMTEST-1 to the plugin, and Unwrap runs through the mock.
exec sesam --identity $PLUGIN_KEY show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.tell"'
stdout '"yubi"'

# Kill 'yubi': rotates the audit key so the plugin identity loses access.
exec sesam kill --user yubi
! exec sesam --identity $PLUGIN_KEY show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
